name: libsub-name-perl
title: libsub-name-perl
summary: module for assigning a new name to referenced sub
description:
module for assigning a new name to referenced sub
Sub::Name has only one function, which is also exported by default:
.
subname NAME, CODEREF
.
Assigns a new name to referenced sub. If package specification is
omitted in the name, then the current package is used. The return
value is the sub.
.
The name is only used for informative routines (caller, Carp, etc).
You won't be able to actually invoke the sub by the given name. To
allow that, you need to do glob-assignment yourself.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use libsub-name-perl because module for assigning a new name to referenced sub.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use libsub-name-perl as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair libsub-name-perl with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `libsub-name-perl --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
